# AppearAnimation

**Object**

An animation type whereby a component appears on the screen.

## Declaration

```swift
object AppearAnimation
```

## Discussion

With this animation type, the component starts out invisible and then appears. As the component enters the person’s view, it appears with a slight fade-in as shown in [https://developer.apple.com/apple-news/videos/appear.mp4].

See also [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/FadeInAnimation], where you can set the initial appearance of the component.

### Example

```json
{
  "components": [
    {
      "role": "heading1",
      "text": "2. Unbeatable Heat"
    },
    {
      "role": "figure",
      "URL": "bundle://figure.jpg",
      "animation": {
        "type": "appear"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Related Documentation

- **Adding Animations**: Use animations to affect how parts of your article come into view the first time they appear.

## Animations

- **About Component Animations**: Learn how to affect the way in which components come into view.
- **ComponentAnimation**: Properties that all types of animations share.
- **FadeInAnimation**: The animation whereby a component fades into view.
- **MoveInAnimation**: The animation whereby a component moves in from the side of the screen.
- **ScaleFadeAnimation**: The animation in which a component scales up and fades into view.

## Inherits From

- ComponentAnimation
